headline: Why East Malaysia Pays More for Everything
context: Cabotage policy restricts Peninsular–East Malaysia shipping to local vessels. A bag of rice costs 30% more in Kota Kinabalu than KL.

cards.big: Same country. Same currency. 30% higher prices.
cards.sub: The shipping policy causing it has been discussed 20 years.

cards.big: RM4.7 billion per year. That's what cabotage costs East Malaysians.
Five Malaysia Plans. Five reviews. Nothing changed.
cards.sub: The 2017 exemption covered 10% of cargo categories.

cards.big: Restricted competition → higher prices → regressive impact.
cards.sub: Not a grievance. A market failure maintained by policy.

cards.big: Does RM4.7B in consumer cost serve a national interest worth RM4.7B?
Diffuse population loses. Concentrated lobby wins. Classic political economy.
